reasoning: WHO declarations of new pandemics are extremely rare historical events. Only one coronavirus pandemic (COVID-19) has ever been declared by the WHO in its history. The discovery of a new bat coronavirus (as reported in the background) is a routine scientific finding and very rarely leads to an immediate spillover event, let alone a global pandemic. Furthermore, based on the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, there are fewer than two months remaining in the year. If a new coronavirus were to be declared a pandemic before the end of December 2025, an escalating global outbreak would already be major international news by early November. Given the absence of such an extraordinary global event, the time remaining makes a pandemic declaration effectively impossible. The probability is bounded to the lowest allowed value (0.02).
